title: GraphSchemata
description: Nested graphs where every Node has a public signature (inputs/outputs/params). Subnets are Nodes that contain child nodes/edges. GraphInput/GraphOutput/GraphProp are special UI boundary adapters generated deterministically from a subnet’s declared inputs/outputs/params. Edges are in-scope only and connect named ports (no indexes/lanes). Channels default to 'main' when omitted.

Port.description: A named, typed port in a node signature. No lanes. Fan-in requires explicit Merge/Collect nodes (or a collection type if you later add that convention).

Ref.description: Houdini-like reference expression. Can be used anywhere a value is allowed (especially Param.value). Rename/move can break refs and that's expected.

Param.description: A configuration parameter (not a dataflow port). Values may be literals or Ref objects.

value.description: Literal JSON value or a Ref object.

EdgeEndpoint.description: In-scope endpoint. node is a relative name/path within the current graph scope. port is a named port (src uses outputs, dst uses inputs).

Edge.description: Connects a source node output port to a destination node input port. No lanes/indexes. Channel defaults to 'main' if omitted.

channel.description: Optional channel namespace (e.g. 'main', 'error', 'control'). Default is 'main' if omitted.

Group.description: UI grouping of sibling nodes in the same graph scope.

NodeKind.description: Optional hint for tooling/UI. Execution semantics are defined by (context, type). GraphInput/GraphOutput are special boundary adapters for subnets.

Node.description: Everything is a Node. A Node with child nodes/edges is a subnet (a folder/graph). The root document is the root Node.

name.description: Node name. By convention may be a path (e.g. 'some/subnet1/node2'). Identity is name-within-parent-scope; rename/move is identity change.

kind.description: Optional. If omitted, tooling may infer 'subnet' when nodes/edges exist; otherwise 'node'.

type.description: Node type key within the given context.

inputs.description: Public input signature. For subnets, these define the external interface and drive generation of GraphInput boundary nodes.

outputs.description: Public output signature. For subnets, these define the external interface and drive generation of GraphOutput boundary nodes.

params.description: Public parameter signature (configuration). Values may be literals or Ref objects.

nodes.description: Child nodes (subgraph). Presence implies this Node is a subnet/folder/graph.

edges.description: Edges connecting child nodes. In-scope only (endpoints must reference sibling nodes in this Node.nodes).

groups.description: Optional UI grouping of child nodes.

x-normative-rules: Identity: A node's identity is its name within its parent scope (paths are allowed by convention). Rename/move is an identity change; references must be rewritten by tooling.
Scope: EdgeEndpoint.node values are relative and must refer to nodes within the same graph scope (i.e., members of the current Node.nodes). No cross-scope edges.
No lanes: Ports are named; there is no index/lane dimension. Branching uses explicit output port names (e.g., true/false/caseA/default/error).
Fan-in: Multiple upstream values must be combined with explicit nodes (Merge/Collect/Join/etc.). No implicit merging into a single-typed input.
Channels: Edge.channel is optional. If omitted, it is treated as 'main'. Only serialize when non-main.
Subnets: Any Node with child nodes/edges is a subnet. Its public interface is defined by its inputs/outputs/params fields.

Boundary adapters (UI): For any subnet, GraphInput, GraphOutput, and GraphProp nodes are generated deterministically from the subnet's declared inputs/outputs/params (Strategy 1). They are special node kinds and not treated as normal nodes.

Deterministic generation: Generated boundary node names and ordering must be stable (e.g., '__in/<portName>', '__out/<portName>', '__prop/<paramName>', sorted by name) to support canonicalization/merkle hashing.

Validation: Edges must connect src.port that exists in src node outputs to dst.port that exists in dst node inputs (or that exists in the node type registry signature for that node's (context, type) if you choose registry-derived ports).
Canonicalization: Consider sorting inputs/outputs/params arrays by name and excluding UI-only metadata fields from hashing to keep content-addressable IDs stable across layout changes.
